---
title: "Auto-generated research report workflow"
subtitle: "Agent planning, human approval, and inputs from literature, skills, and register data"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    embed-resources: true
---

This page describes one way to auto-generate a research report when you combine **PDF background literature** (the corpus under `pdfs/`, catalogued in `chatbots/sources.md`), **chatbot-style custom instructions** in `chatbots/*.md`, and **register-style tabular data with complete metadata** (data plus a dictionary or metadata file, as in external surveys that ship `data.csv` with `metadata.csv`, as in [`KI4Forsking.qmd`](KI4Forsking.qmd)). Methods-oriented personas stress estimand specification, multiplicity, and reproducibility.

Mermaid diagrams render in Quarto **HTML** output out of the box. For **PDF**, ensure your Quarto setup can render Mermaid; otherwise use HTML preview or export.

## Agent skills vs chatbot instructions (Claude / Cursor)

**Two layers:**

1. **Project chatbots (`chatbots/*.md`)** — YAML front matter plus an instructions block. These are the canonical method and writing personas for this repo. In Claude, paste or upload them as project knowledge / custom instructions; in Cursor, paste them into **Rules**, **AGENTS.md**, or convert them into **Agent Skills** (a folder with `SKILL.md`) so the agent loads them when the task matches the skill description.
2. **Cursor Agent Skills** (global or project `.cursor/skills/.../SKILL.md`) — Reusable procedures the IDE can pull in by description. They are not the same files as the chatbots unless you duplicate or wrap a chatbot (for example a skill that says “follow the Methods Consultant — Epidemiology persona” and points to `chatbots/methods_consultant_epidemiology.md`).

### Repo chatbots (skill-equivalent personas)

| File | Role |
|------|------|
| [`chatbots/methods_consultant.md`](chatbots/methods_consultant.md) | General methods, estimand, standard errors, multiplicity, p-values |
| [`chatbots/methods_consultant_epidemiology.md`](chatbots/methods_consultant_epidemiology.md) | DAGs, identifiability, study designs, reporting (e.g. STROBE) |
| [`chatbots/methods_consultant_econometrics.md`](chatbots/methods_consultant_econometrics.md) | Causal econometrics, DiD, IV, RDD, program evaluation |
| [`chatbots/methods_consultant_psychology.md`](chatbots/methods_consultant_psychology.md) | Preregistration, replication, measurement, psychology methods |
| [`chatbots/copy_editor_english.md`](chatbots/copy_editor_english.md) | English clarity and structure |
| [`chatbots/copy_editor_enkelt_sprak.md`](chatbots/copy_editor_enkelt_sprak.md) | Norwegian plain-language editing |
| [`chatbots/grant_reviewer.md`](chatbots/grant_reviewer.md) | Review framing; high-stakes summaries before submission |
| [`chatbots/literature_synthesizer.md`](chatbots/literature_synthesizer.md) | Traceable extraction and synthesis from PDFs; comparison tables |
| [`chatbots/code_writer.md`](chatbots/code_writer.md) | Reproducible R/Python layout, logging, checkpoints, data integrity checks |
| [`chatbots/sap_prereg_drafter.md`](chatbots/sap_prereg_drafter.md) | SAP / OSF-style pre-registration text from an approved plan |
| [`chatbots/NotebookLMCustomInstructions.txt`](chatbots/NotebookLMCustomInstructions.txt) | NotebookLM-specific; port if you want the same behavior elsewhere |
| [`chatbots/sources.md`](chatbots/sources.md) | Index of PDFs (retrieval scope); not a persona |

### Skills by workflow phase (agent side)

| Phase | Primary skill / persona | Secondary |
|-------|---------------------------|-----------|
| 1 Background literature review | `literature_synthesizer.md` plus retrieval over `pdfs/` | Domain methods consultant to critique claims |
| 2 Research goal / estimand | `methods_consultant.md` plus **one** domain file (epidemiology / econometrics / psychology) | Same domain file for identifiability language |
| 3 Analysis plan | Domain methods consultant; `methods_consultant.md` for multiplicity, SEs, estimand checks | `sap_prereg_drafter.md` for registration-ready text |
| 4 Implementation of analysis | `code_writer.md` plus coding and reproducibility; optional **measure-first pipeline debugging** when row counts or tags diverge across stages | Domain consultant to validate models vs estimand |
| 5 Results description | `methods_consultant.md` (effect sizes, CIs, p-values, exploratory vs confirmatory) | Domain consultant for discipline-specific reporting |
| 6 Discussion | Domain methods consultant and literature tie-back | `copy_editor_english.md` or `copy_editor_enkelt_sprak.md`; optional `grant_reviewer.md` before submission |

### Optional Cursor skills (global)

These are not in `chatbots/` but often apply:

- **Measure-first pipeline debugging** — Phase 4 when records or tags disappear between parse, clean, model, and export stages.
- **Babysit PR / CI** — When merging the report or analysis via GitHub and keeping CI green (operational).

### Gaps worth adding (optional `SKILL.md` and/or `chatbots/*.md`)

- **Register data and metadata QA** — Dedicated persona for dictionary validation, cohort flags, missingness codes, linkage rules (Phases 3–4); partly overlaps `code_writer.md` data-integrity bullets.
- **Ethics / DPA / access gate** — Human-first checklist persona; reminds the agent not to mishandle sensitive registers.

---

## Workflow diagram

Each phase follows **plan → human approve (or revise) → agent implement**. Solid arrows chain phases; dashed arrows show extra inputs from PDFs, chatbots/skills, and register metadata.

```{mermaid}
flowchart TB
  subgraph inputs [Inputs]
    PDFs[PDFs background literature]
    Skills[Skills and chatbot instructions]
    Reg[Register data and complete metadata]
  end

  subgraph phase1 [Phase 1 Background literature review]
    A1P["Agent: plan lit review retrieval and outline"]
    H1A["Human: approve or request revision"]
    A1I["Agent: implement structured synthesis with traceable citations"]
    A1P --> H1A --> A1I
    H1A -.->|revise| A1P
  end

  subgraph phase2 [Phase 2 Research goal and estimand]
    A2P["Agent: plan estimand population contrast and measures"]
    H2A["Human: approve or request revision"]
    A2I["Agent: implement estimand text for methods"]
    A2P --> H2A --> A2I
    H2A -.->|revise| A2P
  end

  subgraph phase3 [Phase 3 Analysis plan]
    A3P["Agent: plan pre-spec models covariates sensitivity multiplicity"]
    H3A["Human: approve or request revision"]
    A3I["Agent: implement locked analysis specification"]
    A3P --> H3A --> A3I
    H3A -.->|revise| A3P
  end

  subgraph phase4 [Phase 4 Implementation of analysis]
    A4P["Agent: plan repo layout checks and reproducibility"]
    H4A["Human: approve or request revision"]
    A4I["Agent: implement scripted pipeline from raw data to outputs"]
    A4P --> H4A --> A4I
    H4A -.->|revise| A4P
  end

  subgraph phase5 [Phase 5 Results description]
    A5P["Agent: plan tables and figures tied to estimands"]
    H5A["Human: approve or request revision"]
    A5I["Agent: implement neutral results narrative with CIs and effect sizes"]
    A5P --> H5A --> A5I
    H5A -.->|revise| A5P
  end

  subgraph phase6 [Phase 6 Discussion]
    A6P["Agent: plan limitations and links to prior literature"]
    H6A["Human: approve or request revision"]
    A6I["Agent: implement discussion draft optional copy-edit skill pass"]
    A6P --> H6A --> A6I
    H6A -.->|revise| A6P
  end

  HFinal["Human: final signoff ethics access and submission-ready text"]

  PDFs --> A1P
  Skills --> A1P
  Reg --> A1P

  Skills -.->|inform| A2P
  Skills -.->|inform| A3P
  Skills -.->|inform| A4P
  Reg -.->|inform| A3P
  Reg -.->|inform| A4P
  PDFs -.->|inform| A6P

  A1I --> A2P
  A2I --> A3P
  A3I --> A4P
  A4I --> A5P
  A5I --> A6P
  A6I --> HFinal
```

Phase 4 assumes an approved analysis plan and documented register extracts. Attach the **domain methods consultant** and **`methods_consultant.md`** as early as Phase 2 so estimands and estimators stay aligned; use **`code_writer.md`** for implementation conventions and **measure-first debugging** if pipeline stages disagree on counts or labels.
